relatedQ.title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted (LC #167)
relatedQ.problem: Given a SORTED array, find two numbers that add to target. Return 1-indexed positions.
relatedQ.why: Because the array is sorted, we use Two Pointers instead of a hash map — no extra memory needed! Move left pointer right if sum is too small, move right pointer left if too big.

relatedQ.title: Valid Palindrome (LC #125)
relatedQ.problem: Check if a string is a palindrome, considering only alphanumeric characters, ignoring cases.
relatedQ.why: Same palindrome concept but for strings with spaces/punctuation. We filter out non-alphanumeric characters first, then compare.

relatedQ.title: Minimum Remove to Make Valid Parentheses (LC #1249)
relatedQ.problem: Remove minimum parentheses to make the string valid. Return the resulting string.
relatedQ.why: Extension of stack idea — track unmatched brackets by their index, then remove them.

relatedQ.title: Merge Sorted Array (LC #88)
relatedQ.problem: Merge two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 into nums1 in-place.
relatedQ.why: Same merge idea but for arrays. Fill from the END to avoid overwriting unprocessed elements.

relatedQ.title: Remove Element (LC #27)
relatedQ.problem: Remove all occurrences of val in-place from nums. Return new length.
relatedQ.why: Same two-pointer pattern. Instead of skipping duplicates, we skip the specific value.

relatedQ.title: Binary Search (LC #704)
relatedQ.problem: Given a sorted array and target, return index of target or -1 if not found.
relatedQ.solution:
def search(nums, target):
    left, right = 0, len(nums) - 1
    while left <= right:
        mid = (left + right) // 2
        if nums[mid] == target: return mid
        elif nums[mid] < target: left = mid + 1
        else: right = mid - 1
    return -1
relatedQ.why: Pure binary search — same logic but returns -1 instead of insert position when not found.

relatedQ.title: Reverse Words in a String (LC #151)
relatedQ.problem: Reverse the order of words in a string. Handle multiple spaces.
relatedQ.why: Same split() trick, then reverse the word list with [::-1], then join with single spaces.

relatedQ.title: Add Binary (LC #67)
relatedQ.problem: Given two binary strings a and b, return their sum as a binary string.
relatedQ.solution:
def addBinary(a, b):
    return bin(int(a, 2) + int(b, 2))[2:]
relatedQ.why: Convert binary strings to integers, add, convert back. int(a, 2) reads base-2 string. bin() converts to binary. [2:] removes '0b' prefix.

relatedQ.title: Remove Linked List Elements (LC #203)
relatedQ.problem: Remove all nodes with a specific value from the linked list.
relatedQ.why: Same skip technique, but uses a dummy node to handle removing the head node cleanly.

relatedQ.title: Merge Two Sorted Lists (LC #21)
relatedQ.problem: Merge two sorted linked lists into one sorted list.
relatedQ.why: Same merge logic but for linked lists where we can't fill backwards — we build a new list from front.

relatedQ.title: Symmetric Tree (LC #101)
relatedQ.problem: Check if a binary tree is symmetric (mirror of itself).
relatedQ.why: Same recursive structure, but we compare left.left with right.right and left.right with right.left (mirror check).

relatedQ.title: Minimum Depth of Binary Tree (LC #111)
relatedQ.problem: Return the minimum depth (shortest path from root to a leaf).
relatedQ.why: Similar but use min() instead of max(). Special handling needed for nodes with only one child — they can't be a leaf.

relatedQ.problem: You can buy/sell multiple times. Maximize total profit.
relatedQ.why: With unlimited transactions, simply grab every upward movement (local profit). No need to track min/max across the whole array.

relatedQ.title: Missing Number (LC #268)
relatedQ.problem: Given [0..n] with one number missing, find it.
relatedQ.why: Expected sum - actual sum = missing number. XOR approach also works: XOR all indices 0..n with all nums.

relatedQ.title: Implement Queue using Stacks (LC #232)
relatedQ.problem: Implement a FIFO queue using only stack operations.
relatedQ.why: Two-stack technique: use two stacks to simulate queue (FIFO). Push to in_stack; when out_stack empty, pour in_stack into out_stack (reverses order → FIFO).

relatedQ.title: Linked List Cycle (LC #141)
relatedQ.problem: Detect if a linked list has a cycle.
relatedQ.why: Floyd's cycle detection (slow/fast pointers). Fast moves 2x. If there's a cycle, fast will eventually lap slow and they'll meet.

relatedQ.title: Find Mode in Binary Search Tree (LC #501)
relatedQ.problem: Find mode(s) in a BST (values appearing most frequently).
relatedQ.why: Counter from collections module counts frequencies efficiently. Then filter for keys with max frequency.

relatedQ.title: Palindrome Linked List (LC #234)
relatedQ.problem: Check if a linked list is a palindrome.
relatedQ.why: Collect values into a list, then check if list equals its reverse. Simple approach using reverseList logic conceptually.

relatedQ.title: Contains Duplicate II (LC #219)
relatedQ.problem: Return true if same value appears within k positions of each other.
relatedQ.why: Hash map stores last seen index. For each number, check if previously seen within k distance.

relatedQ.title: Group Anagrams (LC #49) — Medium
relatedQ.problem: Group a list of strings into anagram groups.
relatedQ.why: Sort each string to get a canonical 'anagram key'. Group strings by their sorted form using a defaultdict.

relatedQ.title: Find the Duplicate Number (LC #287) — Medium
relatedQ.problem: Find the duplicate number in array [1..n] with one duplicate.
relatedQ.solution:
def findDuplicate(nums):
    slow = fast = nums[0]
    while True:
        slow = nums[slow]
        fast = nums[nums[fast]]
        if slow == fast: break
    slow = nums[0]
    while slow != fast:
        slow = nums[slow]
        fast = nums[fast]
    return slow
relatedQ.why: Floyd's cycle detection on the array interpreted as a linked list. The duplicate creates the cycle's entry point.

relatedQ.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array (LC #26)
relatedQ.problem: Remove duplicates in-place from a sorted array.
relatedQ.why: Same two-pointer write-position pattern. Instead of filtering zeros, filtering duplicates.

relatedQ.title: Reverse Vowels of a String (LC #345)
relatedQ.problem: Reverse only the vowels of a string.
relatedQ.why: Same two-pointer swap, but skip non-vowels. Add inner while loops to advance pointers past consonants.

relatedQ.title: Intersection of Two Arrays (LC #349)
relatedQ.problem: Return the intersection (unique elements only).
relatedQ.solution:
def intersection(nums1, nums2):
    return list(set(nums1) & set(nums2))
relatedQ.why: Set intersection (&) gives common unique elements directly. Simpler when duplicates don't matter.

relatedQ.title: Number of Students Unable to Eat Lunch (LC #1700)
relatedQ.problem: Circular queue of students, stack of sandwiches. Return students who can't eat.
relatedQ.why: Count student preferences. If the top sandwich can't be served (count=0), remaining students can't eat.

RELATED_QUESTIONS.title: Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array

relatedQ.title: Find the Duplicate Number (LC #287) — Medium
relatedQ.problem: Find the one duplicate in array [1..n].
relatedQ.why: Same negation trick but detect when we try to negate an already-negative index — that's the duplicate!

relatedQ.title: Climbing Stairs (LC #70) — same as Fibonacci!
relatedQ.problem: Count ways to climb n stairs taking 1 or 2 steps at a time.
relatedQ.why: Climbing stairs IS Fibonacci! Ways(n) = Ways(n-1) + Ways(n-2). Identical recurrence relation.

relatedQ.title: Search Insert Position (LC #35)
relatedQ.problem: Find target or return where it would be inserted.
relatedQ.solution:
def searchInsert(nums, target):
    l, r = 0, len(nums) - 1
    while l <= r:
        m = (l + r) // 2
        if nums[m] == target: return m
        elif nums[m] < target: l = m + 1
        else: r = m - 1
    return l  # insert position
relatedQ.why: Identical binary search, but return 'l' instead of -1 when not found — 'l' is exactly where target would be inserted.

relatedQ.title: Linked List Cycle (LC #141)
relatedQ.problem: Detect if a linked list has a cycle.
relatedQ.solution:
def hasCycle(head):
    slow = fast = head
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
        if slow == fast: return True
    return False
relatedQ.why: Same slow/fast pointer idea. In a cycle, fast will eventually lap slow and they'll meet at the same node.

relatedQ.title: Sort Array By Parity (LC #905)
relatedQ.problem: Move even numbers to front, odd to back.
relatedQ.solution:
def sortArrayByParity(nums):
    l, r = 0, len(nums) - 1
    while l < r:
        while l < r and nums[l] % 2 == 0: l += 1
        while l < r and nums[r] % 2 == 1: r -= 1
        nums[l], nums[r] = nums[r], nums[l]
        l += 1; r -= 1
    return nums
relatedQ.why: Two-pointer swap. Skip even on left, skip odd on right. When both are 'wrong side', swap them.

relatedQ.title: Add Two Numbers (LC #2) — Medium
relatedQ.problem: Add two numbers represented by linked lists.
relatedQ.solution: Similar carry logic but with linked list nodes.
relatedQ.why: Linked lists can be added node-by-node just like adding bits in a string.

relatedQ.title: Valid Perfect Square (LC #367)
relatedQ.problem: Check if a number is a perfect square without using sqrt().
relatedQ.solution: Same binary search logic, but return True if pivot*pivot == x.
relatedQ.why: The search pattern is identical — finding an integer k such that k*k = x.

relatedQ.title: Min Cost Climbing Stairs (LC #746)
relatedQ.problem: You pay cost[i] to step on step i. Find minimum cost to reach top.
relatedQ.why: Exact same stair logic, but we track minimum cost instead of total ways.

relatedQ.title: Binary Tree Preorder Traversal (LC #144)
relatedQ.problem: Root → Left → Right traversal.
relatedQ.why: Preorder visits the root first, useful for creating a copy of the tree.

relatedQ.title: Same Tree (LC #100)

relatedQ.solution: Same recursive comparison, but compare left-to-left and right-to-right.

relatedQ.title: Path Sum II (LC #113) — Medium

relatedQ.title: Linked List Cycle II (LC #142) — Medium
relatedQ.problem: Return the node where the cycle begins.
relatedQ.solution: After slow catches fast, move slow to head and move both at speed 1. They meet at entry.

relatedQ.why: Another example where basic string manipulation is required.

relatedQ.title: Longest Common Subsequence (LC #1143) — Medium
relatedQ.problem: Find length of common items.
relatedQ.why: The complex version of this problem where strings ARE usually shared.

relatedQ.title: Valid BST (LC #98) — Medium
relatedQ.problem: Check if tree is valid BST.
relatedQ.why: Both rely on the fundamental 'sorted' property of in-order traversal in BSTs.

relatedQ.title: First Unique Character (LC #387)
relatedQ.problem: Find first non-repeating char.
relatedQ.why: Frequent pattern: checking properties/counts of chars in a stream.

RELATED_QUESTIONS.title: Reverse Words in a String III

relatedQ.title: Reverse String (LC #344)
relatedQ.problem: Reverse array in place.
relatedQ.why: Basic building block of this multi-word problem.

relatedQ.title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree (LC #104)
relatedQ.problem: Find height of 2-child tree.
relatedQ.why: Specifically limited to 2 children instead of N.

relatedQ.title: Maximize Sum After K Negations (LC #1005)
relatedQ.problem: Modify array to max sum.
relatedQ.why: Both use sorting to make greedy local decisions that lead to global maximum.

relatedQ.title: Subtree of Another Tree (LC #572)
relatedQ.problem: Is tree A inside B?
relatedQ.why: Another example of using subtree properties to answer a global question.

relatedQ.title: Transpose Matrix (LC #867)
relatedQ.problem: Swap rows/cols.
relatedQ.why: The other common matrix re-orientation problem.
